articles.summary: Iran’s foreign minister calls actions of French diplomats ‘unacceptable’ Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Tuesday in a call to his French counterpart Jean-Noel Barrot that the conduct of two French diplomats in Tehran was “unconventional”, “unprofessional” and “unacceptable”. This comes after Barrot warned of “consequences” for Iranian security services detaining two of their diplomats on Monday.

articles.summary: Trump: US will hit rumoured Iranian nuclear site ‘soon’ Trump told reporters on Tuesday that the US will be hitting the area where Iran’s nuclear centrifuges are rumoured to be “very heavily” in the near future, without clarifying when. “Probably pretty soon, and there’s not a thing they can do about it,” he said following a meeting with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun in the White House. Pickaxe Mountain is said to be the site where Iran’s nuclear programme was moved to, located near Iran’s Natanz uranium enrichment facility in the south of Tehran. articles.title: Ed Miliband, the new UK foreign secretary, on Israel, Iraq, Syria and Iran
articles.summary: Ed Miliband, the new UK foreign secretary, on Israel, Iraq, Syria and Iran Having initially been tipped for the role of chancellor, former Labour leader Ed Miliband has been appointed foreign secretary by Andy Burnham, the UK's new prime minister. Accepting the role, Miliband said that it was an “honour and privilege” to take up the position. “My parents came to Britain as Jewish refugees from the Nazis,” he said.  “To them, Britain was both a sanctuary and a beacon of hope in the global fight against fascism. articles.title: US announces trade deal with Jordan
articles.summary: US announces trade deal with Jordan The US announced on Tuesday that they will be entering a trade deal with Jordan which would remove barriers for US exporters and provide new access for critical US industries.  According to a statement from the White House, Jordan will remove non-tariff trade barriers and expand market access for US goods, including agricultural products and motor vehicles, as part of the deal. This comes amid renewed Iranian strikes on Jordan, which killed two US military personnel on Friday.

The so-called Yellow Line, introduced on 10 October, became the main reference point for the areas where Israeli forces remained deployed.
